checks.finding: REQ-001 through REQ-005 define their relevant inputs, ordered decisions, failure behavior, protected inventory, and observable outcomes sufficiently for subsequent design.

The requirements and acceptance tests consistently preserve write denial, force full-track escalation, require generated-module drift checks, and assign protected live writes to the human-copy workflow.

The final Risks bullet states that a rename-time OS failure can leave an installed inventory prefix and requires a documented reviewed rollback batch, while AC-013 does not cover an injected mid-sequence rename failure, identification of the installed prefix, execution of rollback, or verification of the restored live inventory.
The assumptions are bounded and testable, including the Windows PowerShell 5.1 Full Language, local drive, and NTFS compatibility boundary.
Roles and workflows separate agent staging from human verification and live protected copying, while CI is check-only.
The required reviewed rollback batch after a partial installation has no acceptance criterion, so downstream task decomposition and quality verification lack defined restoration evidence.
